title: Respiratory Surge Oxygen Allocation
description: An acute-care operations workflow that coordinates urgent intake, prioritization, and constrained resource assignment under clinical safety rules. Focus case: respiratory surge oxygen allocation.
assumptions: All resources and staff are modeled as discrete, trackable entities with atomic availability predicates.
Compatibility between patients and resources is precomputed and encoded in pairwise predicates (no on-the-fly compatibility resolution).
Clinician approvals and safety checks are required before irreversible resource consumption or final therapy activation.
Inventory state updates and reservation changes are reliable and reflect real-world physical changes (no silent failures).
Single-patient exclusivity for resource_batch and single-use consumables is enforced by the workflow.
risks: Resource exhaustion under surge conditions leading to delayed therapy or unsafe substitutions.
Incorrect compatibility mapping (false-positive compatibility) causing unsafe device-patient pairings.
Delays in clinician availability creating bottlenecks at approval gates and increasing occupancy time.
Improper release of resources (failure to return to inventory) leading to phantom shortages.
Concurrent reservation races if external systems update inventory without transactional synchronization.
